fact: Ryan Morrison, xQc's agent and CEO of the Evolved Talent Agency, publicly called xQc's ~$100M Kick deal 'one of the top 25 talent deals of all time period, including sports.' The deal — worth a reported $70M base with incentives up to $100M — was brokered by Morrison and announced in June 2023. The quote is confirmed verbatim by PC Gamer (June 16, 2023), Xfire (June 19, 2023), and multiple other outlets, and is still actively cited in 2025–2026 coverage of xQc's earnings.

When xQc first announced his move to Kick in June 2023, the platform saw significant growth: the number of active streamers quadrupled and hours watched increased by 44% (to more than 84 million), per Streams Charts data. The deal was brokered by Ryan Morrison, CEO of Evolved Talent Agency.

'I repped the largest male and female on Twitch for five years. Not once did they reach out to ask what they think or get feedback on something. Kick, that happens daily.' — Ryan Morrison, CEO, Evolved Talent Agency, speaking to Campaign US and PRWeek at TwitchCon, October 2023. Published at campaignlive.com/article/xqc-amouranth-kitbogas-talent-agent-kick-vs-twitch-brand-deals/1845857.

'But what I personally look for in a brand partner is, they don't necessarily have to understand the space, but they have to understand that they don't understand the space. That means allowing the creator to promote the product in a way that will genuinely allow the audience to absorb what the product is.' — Ryan Morrison, CEO of Evolved Talent Agency, speaking to Campaign US and PRWeek at TwitchCon, October 2023. Source: https://www.campaignlive.com/article/xqc-amouranth-kitbogas-talent-agent-kick-vs-twitch-brand-deals/1845857

Ryan Morrison, CEO of Evolved Talent Agency, said at TwitchCon (October 2023): 'That happened in DMs, and he got a scolding, so no more of that' — referring to the only brand deal xQc ever did without Morrison personally negotiating it: a gambling deal made directly through Kick owner Stake. Source: Campaign Live, October 31, 2023 (https://www.campaignlive.com/article/xqc-amouranth-kitbogas-talent-agent-kick-vs-twitch-brand-deals/1845857), corroborated by PRWeek (October 30, 2023).

Evolved was formed in December 2016 by a group of high-profile industry members who came together seeking fairer treatment of esports players. The agency was founded with the explicit objective of transforming esports player rights globally and setting a high standard for talent representation. This is confirmed by The Esports Observer (Dec 20, 2016), VentureBeat (Dec 21, 2016), Fortune (Dec 22, 2016), the EverybodyWiki article, and Evolved's own LinkedIn company page.

Evolved CEO Ryan Morrison, also known as 'the Video Game Attorney,' is a Founding Partner of MGL LLP (formerly Morrison Rothman LLP, then Morrison Cooper LLP), a boutique law firm specializing in esports, video games, and digital entertainment law, headquartered in Los Angeles. The firm's current name and website is mgllaw.com. The 'Morrison Cooper LLP' name in the candidate is stale — the firm now operates as MGL LLP.
